c74object.name: stoplight
c74object.digest: A data gate for Max messages
c74object.description: The `stoplight` object is a data gate that either passes through incoming messages or queues them based on a control signal. When the control inlet (Inlet 1) is 0, messages from Inlet 0 are passed immediately to the outlet. When the control signal is non-zero, incoming messages are stored in a FIFO (First-In-First-Out) queue. As soon as the control signal returns to 0, all queued messages are released in the order they were received.

inlet.digest: Inlet 0: Data to be passed or queued.
inlet.description: Accepts any Max message. These messages are either passed immediately to the outlet or stored in a FIFO queue depending on the state of the control inlet.

A control value. When 0 (or if no value has been received), Inlet 0 acts as a passthrough. When non-zero, incoming data from Inlet 0 is queued. If a non-zero value changes back to 0, the queue is immediately flushed.

outlet.description: The data output from the gate. This will either be a real-time passthrough of messages from Inlet 0, or a sequential flush of queued messages.
